project.name: AEM Dispatcher Filter Fiddler
project.description: A browser-based learning and prototyping tool that accepts raw HTTP request lines and auto-generates AEM Dispatcher module filter rules. Intended as a syntax reference and educational aid, not a production rule generator.
project.audience: Systems integrators building AEM Dispatcher farm filter configurations

purpose.primary_goal: Help systems integrators understand and correctly author AEM Dispatcher /filter rules by demonstrating how to use all available matchers together rather than relying on overly-broad /glob allow rules.
learning_outcomes: Understand the syntax and available matchers for AEM Dispatcher filter rules
See how a real HTTP request maps to specific filter matchers: /method, /path, /url, /extension, /selectors, /suffix, /query
Understand the security implications of /glob allow rules vs. specific matcher-based rules
Understand the difference between single-quoted (regex) and double-quoted (glob) matcher values
purpose.disclaimer: This is a learning tool. Generated rules are starting-point syntax examples. Users must review, refine, and test rules before using them in production.

aem_dispatcher.description: Apache httpd module that acts as a caching and load-balancing reverse proxy in front of AEM (Adobe Experience Manager) publish instances. Filter rules control which HTTP requests are forwarded to AEM.

filter_rule_structure.description: Each filter rule is a named block inside /filter { } in the dispatcher.any farm file.

/type.description: Whether the rule permits or blocks matching requests. Best practice is a base deny-all rule followed by specific allow rules.

/method.description: Matches the HTTP method (GET, POST, HEAD, etc.). Use double-quoted glob or single-quoted regex.

/url.description: Matches the full URL path including selectors, extension, and suffix (everything before the query string). More permissive than /path + /extension combined.

/path.description: Matches only the resource path segment (before selectors and extension). More precise than /url alone.

/extension.description: Matches the file extension without the leading dot.

/selectors.description: Matches AEM selectors (the dot-separated segments between resource name and extension). Required to restrict which Sling renderers are accessible.

/suffix.description: Matches the URL suffix (path segment after the extension, prefixed with a slash). Used for Sling URL suffix patterns.

/query.description: Matches query string parameters. Single-valued uses glob; multi-valued uses regex alternation.

/glob.description: DEPRECATED for /allow rules. Matches the entire request URI with a glob pattern. Extremely dangerous to use with /type allow because a single rule can inadvertently allow unintended paths.
/glob.security_warning: NEVER use /glob with /type allow. It cannot restrict by extension, selectors, or method simultaneously and makes it trivial to accidentally expose admin endpoints or sensitive content paths.

double_quotes.matching: Glob pattern matching. Wildcards: * matches any sequence within a path segment, ** matches across segments.

single_quotes.matching: POSIX Extended Regular Expression (ERE) matching. Full regex syntax available.

security_best_practices: Always start with a deny-all rule: /0001 { /type "deny" /url "*" }
Use the most specific combination of matchers possible for each allow rule
Always specify /method to limit to necessary HTTP verbs
Restrict /extension to prevent accessing internal Sling renderers (e.g., .infinity.json, .tidy.json)
Restrict /selectors to only those required by the application
Use /path together with /url for maximum specificity
Rule numbers are significant: rules are evaluated in order; lower numbers evaluated first

rule_numbering_convention.description: Rules are prefixed with /0XXX where XXX is a zero-padded number. Evaluation order matches numbering order.

common_conventions.3000+: Application-specific allows (range used by this tool)

tool_behavior.input: One or more raw HTTP request lines, one per line, in the format: METHOD /path/resource.selector.extension/suffix?query=value
tool_behavior.output: One AEM Dispatcher filter rule block per non-empty input line, numbered sequentially starting at /03000

parsing_logic.extension: Extracted via regex from the URL before a slash (suffix) or at end of path before query string
parsing_logic.method: First whitespace-terminated token on the line
parsing_logic.path: Directory portion of the URL up to the resource name, with trailing wildcard appended
parsing_logic.selectors: Dot-separated segments between resource name and extension
parsing_logic.suffix: URL segment after the extension and before the query string
parsing_logic.query: Parameters after ? split on & into individual key=value pairs; values replaced with wildcard

tool_behavior.live_generation: Rules regenerate on every keystroke/paste event in the input textarea

ui.layout: Two stacked textareas: top for HTTP request input, bottom for generated dispatcher rules

known_limitations: Generated rules are starting-point examples only — real rules need manual review and testing
The tool does not validate whether a generated rule is secure or complete
Selector and query wildcard substitution is heuristic-based and may not match all edge cases
Rule deduplication and merging must be done manually
URL wildcard appending (*) in /url and /path may be overly broad for some use cases
